reasoning_model_1: Model 1 accurately speaks the text '欢迎大家，快进来坐，别客气' without any pronunciation errors. The audio is fluent and free of noise. However, regarding the core requirement '热情' (enthusiastic attitude), the performance is lacking. The delivery is quite slow and measured (0.0s-3.5s), resembling a formal broadcast or a standard reading style rather than a genuine, warm greeting. The tone is polite but flat, lacking the energy, pitch variation, and brightness associated with enthusiasm. It fails to convey the requested emotion effectively, sounding more neutral/polite than enthusiastic.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 accurately follows the text script. The speech is very natural and fluent. Regarding the '热情' (enthusiastic) attitude, the model performs excellently. From the beginning (0.0s-0.8s), the voice has a higher pitch and brighter timbre. The middle segment '快进来坐' (0.8s-1.8s) is spoken with a quicker pace and dynamic intonation, effectively simulating the urgency and warmth of welcoming guests. The ending '别客气' maintains this friendly and high-energy vibe. The emotion is vivid and spot-on.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner in this comparison. While both models produce fluent and text-accurate speech, they differ significantly in the expression of the required '热情' (enthusiastic) attitude. Model 1 provides a standard, somewhat flat delivery that sounds like a formal announcement or a default TTS voice; it is polite but lacks energy. Model 2, in contrast, uses appropriate prosodic features—such as faster pacing, higher pitch, and brighter tonal quality—to genuinely convey excitement and warmth. Model 2 strictly adheres to the paralinguistic instruction, whereas Model 1 misses the mark on the emotional dimension.
